pre_game.message: Hey there, I'm F. This is all a bit overwhelming, isn't it? I'm just trying to clear my head before things start—how are you feeling about the game?
Nice to meet you, F. I'm H. It's definitely a lot to take in, but I'm feeling pretty focused—I've got a lot riding on this for my family. How are you holding up?
I hear you, H. I’m in a similar boat—got people back home I really want to help out, so I’m just trying to stay as steady as I can.
I appreciate that. Keeping a level head is probably the best strategy we've got right now. Do you have any idea what kind of game we're looking at?
Not really, I just know it involves a shared prize pool, so I'm guessing we'll have to make some tough decisions about how to split it.
That makes sense. I just hope the decisions don't turn into a free-for-all; I'd rather we find a way to make it work for everyone.
I completely agree. It’d be a lot better for all of us if we can stay organized and fair instead of letting things get chaotic.
Definitely. I'm glad we're on the same page—it's good to know there's someone else here looking to keep things civil. Have you talked to many of the others yet?
Not really, just some brief introductions. It feels like everyone is still a bit guarded, so I’m glad we could actually talk.

Same here, F. It’s definitely nice to find a friendly face early on. I’m going to go mingle and see where everyone else’s heads are at, but let’s try to keep that cooperative spirit once things start.
Sounds like a plan, H. Let's look out for each other once things get moving. Good luck talking with the others.

post_game.message: Great coordination! I'm glad we all stuck to the $10,000 fair share and reached the best possible outcome.
I agree, it was a great outcome. I'm glad we could coordinate so effectively for a fair result.
